UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a generic user profile within the system. This entity is a base for clients, barbers, and administrators, linking to an external authentication system for credentials.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

email.description: The email address of the user, used for communication.

phone.description: The phone number of the user.

role.description: The primary role of the user in the application (e.g., 'client', 'barber', 'admin').

isActive.description: Indicates if the user's profile is currently active.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Barber.title: Barber
Barber.description: Represents a barber working at the barbershop, with specific details related to their professional profile and commission.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Barber entity.

userProfileId.description: Reference to the UserProfile associated with this barber. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:1 Barber)

bio.description: A short biography or description of the barber's experience and style.

specialties.description: A list of specialized services or styles the barber excels in.

commissionRate.description: The commission rate (as a percentage, e.g., 0.3 for 30%) applied to services performed by this barber.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the barber's profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the barber's profile was last updated.

Client.title: Client
Client.description: Represents a customer of the barbershop, storing their basic information and preferences.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Client entity.

userProfileId.description: Reference to the UserProfile associated with this client. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:1 Client)

notes.description: Internal notes added by barbers regarding client preferences or history (e.g., 'Cliente prefere a máquina 1 na lateral').

preferredBarberId.description: Reference to the client's preferred barber, if any. (Relationship: Barber 1:N Client)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the client's profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the client's profile was last updated.

Service.title: Service
Service.description: Defines a service offered by the barbershop, including its details, pricing, and duration.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Service entity.

name.description: The name of the service (e.g., 'Corte Masculino', 'Barba', 'Degradê').

description.description: A detailed description of what the service entails.

durationMinutes.description: The estimated duration of the service in minutes.

isActive.description: Indicates if the service is currently offered by the barbershop.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the service was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the service was last updated.

ServiceImage.title: ServiceImage
ServiceImage.description: Stores image URLs and metadata for a specific service, allowing for a portfolio or gallery display.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ServiceImage entity.

serviceId.description: Reference to the Service this image belongs to. (Relationship: Service 1:N ServiceImage)

imageUrl.description: The URL of the image, hosted externally.

altText.description: Alternative text for the image, for accessibility purposes.

order.description: The display order of the image within the service's gallery.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the service image was uploaded.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the service image details were last updated.

BarberAvailability.title: BarberAvailability
BarberAvailability.description: Defines the specific time slots or periods when a barber is available or blocked for appointments. Can represent regular working hours, one-off availability, or specific blocked times.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BarberAvailability entity.

barberId.description: Reference to the Barber whose availability is being defined. (Relationship: Barber 1:N BarberAvailability)

type.description: The type of availability: 'regular' for weekly recurring hours, 'one-off-available' for a specific day's custom schedule, or 'blocked' for a period when the barber is unavailable.

dayOfWeek.description: The day of the week for 'regular' availability (0 for Sunday, 6 for Saturday). Required if 'type' is 'regular'.

date.description: The specific date for 'one-off-available' or 'blocked' periods. Required if 'type' is 'one-off-available' or 'blocked'.

startTime.description: The start time of the availability slot or blocked period (e.g., '09:00').

endTime.description: The end time of the availability slot or blocked period (e.g., '18:00').

description.description: Optional description for the availability, especially useful for 'blocked' periods (e.g., 'Lunch break', 'Vacation').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the availability record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the availability record was last updated.

Appointment.title: Appointment
Appointment.description: Records a specific scheduled appointment between a client and a barber for one or more services.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Appointment entity.

clientId.description: Reference to the Client who booked the appointment. (Relationship: Client 1:N Appointment)

barberId.description: Reference to the Barber assigned to the appointment. (Relationship: Barber 1:N Appointment)

serviceIds.description: References to the Services included in this appointment. (Relationship: Service N:N Appointment)

dateTime.description: The scheduled start date and time of the appointment.

durationMinutes.description: The total calculated duration of the appointment in minutes, based on selected services.

totalPrice.description: The total calculated price of the appointment, based on selected services.

status.description: The current status of the appointment.

notes.description: Optional notes related to the specific appointment.

commissionPaid.description: Indicates whether the barber's commission for this appointment has been processed.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the appointment was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the appointment was last updated.

Expense.title: Expense
Expense.description: Tracks operational expenses for the barbershop to aid in financial management and profit calculation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Expense entity.

amount.description: The monetary amount of the expense.

date.description: The date when the expense was incurred.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile (likely an Admin) who recorded this expense. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Expense)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the expense record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the expense record was last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles (clients, barbers, admins). The `userId` matches `request.auth.uid`. The `role` field indicates the user's primary role, with a separate `/admins` collection providing authoritative admin status for security rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: A collection used for role-based access control. The existence of a document here (where `adminId` matches `request.auth.uid`) grants administrator privileges. This ensures Authorization Independence for role checks by avoiding `get()` calls to other collections.

params.name: adminId
params.description: The unique ID of the admin user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Stores professional profiles for barbers. The `barberId` matches `request.auth.uid` (and `UserProfile.id`). Publicly readable, but only the specific barber or an admin can modify their own profile. The `userProfileId` field in the schema is implicitly represented by the document `id`.

params.name: barberId
params.description: The unique ID of the barber, matching `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Stores customer profiles. The `clientId` matches `request.auth.uid` (and `UserProfile.id`). Clients can modify their own profile, barbers can read specific client profiles to view notes, and admins can manage all. The `userProfileId` field in the schema is implicitly represented by the document `id`.

params.name: clientId
params.description: The unique ID of the client, matching `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Central catalog of services offered by the barbershop. These documents are publicly readable, allowing clients to browse available services. Only administrators can create, update, or delete services.

params.name: serviceId
params.description: The unique ID of the service.

definition.description: Stores gallery images associated with a specific service. Documents include a denormalized `serviceId` for authorization independence. Publicly readable for portfolio display, admin-writable.

params.name: serviceId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent service.

params.name: imageId
params.description: The unique ID of the service image.

definition.description: Defines specific time slots, regular working hours, or blocked periods for a barber. Documents include a denormalized `barberId` for authorization independence. Clients can read to check availability, while the specific barber or an admin can manage these records.

params.name: barberId
params.description: The unique ID of the barber whose availability is defined.

params.name: availabilityId
params.description: The unique ID of the availability record.

definition.description: Records scheduled appointments between clients and barbers. Each document explicitly contains `clientId` and `barberId` fields. This is critical for Authorization Independence, allowing security rules to grant access if `request.auth.uid` matches either ID, enabling secure `list` operations (QAPs) for both clients and barbers.

params.name: appointmentId
params.description: The unique ID of the appointment.

definition.description: Tracks operational expenses for the barbershop. Documents include `userId` (of the admin who recorded it). Access is restricted to administrators only, with authorization based on the `/admins/{adminId}` role marker.

params.name: expenseId
params.description: The unique ID of the expense record.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure is designed to be highly secure, scalable, and debuggable, adhering to all core principles and design mandates.  

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**  
This design achieves Authorization Independence primarily through two mechanisms:  
1.  **Denormalization of Authorization Context:** Every document requiring specific user-based authorization (e.g., appointments, barber availability) explicitly includes the `clientId`, `barberId`, or `userId` field directly within the document. For example, an `Appointment` document stores both `clientId` and `barberId`. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls in security rules to retrieve related parent or user profile data for authorization decisions. Rules can simply compare `request.auth.uid` against these embedded fields. This ensures atomic operations, improves performance, and makes rules significantly easier to write and debug as each document carries its full authorization context.  
2.  **Role Management via Existence Checks:** Instead of relying on `get()` calls to fetch a `UserProfile` and check its `role` field, dedicated role collections like `/admins/{adminId}` are used. The presence of a document in `/admins/{adminId}` (where `adminId` is `request.auth.uid`) serves as a direct, atomic, and authorization-independent check for admin privileges. This approach is superior for rule performance and reliability, preventing `get()` call limitations that can break transactions or batch writes.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**  
Secure `list` operations are enabled by applying **Structural Segregation (Homogeneous Security Posture)**.  
*   Each top-level collection is designed to hold documents with a uniform security posture. For instance, `/services` is publicly readable, while `/appointments` allows access only to the `clientId` or `barberId` associated with that specific appointment.  
*   This segregation means that a query (`db.collection('appointments').where('barberId', '==', 'someBarberId')`) will only attempt to return documents for which the user *already has read permission* according to the security rules. The rules don't act as filters on potentially unauthorized data, which is a fundamental requirement for QAPs.  
*   For example, a barber can query `/appointments` using `where("barberId", "==", request.auth.uid)`. The security rules for `/appointments/{appointmentId}` will independently verify that `request.auth.uid` matches the `barberId` field in *each* document, ensuring only authorized results are returned. Similarly for clients querying by `clientId`.  

**Other Principles and Mandates:**  
*   **Private Data:** User-owned data like `UserProfile`, `Barber`, and `Client` profiles are stored in collections where the document ID matches `request.auth.uid`, establishing clear ownership.  
*   **Hierarchical Paths:** Nested data like `BarberAvailability` is placed as a subcollection under `/barbers/{barberId}`, reinforcing the natural data hierarchy and simplifying ownership-based rules. `ServiceImages` are similarly nested under `Service`.  
*   **Data Clarity:** Explicit state modeling (e.g., `Appointment.status`) and predictable schemas with consistent naming conventions (e.g., `ownerId`, descriptive wildcards like `{barberId}`) are enforced to enhance debuggability and maintainability. This comprehensive approach ensures a robust, secure, and efficient Firestore structure for the barbershop application.
